titleStmt.title: Translation of DCA DCD Misc. Ch. 590

p.seg: William, by the grace of God king of the Scots, to all worthy men of his entire land, clerk and lay, greeting.

Let present and future know me to have allowed, and by this my charter to have made firm, the renuntiation and abjuration which William of Gullane with Nicholas and John his brothers and Emma his maternal aunt made for himself and his entire kindred of the toun of greater Lumsdaine with everything belonging to it, and that which he lawfully held in the same toun, in return for one ploughgate of land granted to him and his heirs in the territory of Swinton by the prior and convent of Durham. Wherefore I will and resolutely instruct that the renuntiation and abjuration made by him be strictly obeserved in future, just as William's own charter bears witness, saving my service from the aforesaid lands.
As witnesses: William bishop of St Andrews, Philip de Valognes my chamberlain, Robert of London my son, William Comyn, Alexander the sheriff of Stirling.
At Wedale on the 16th day of May.
